name: United Kingdom
summary: UK NHS-oriented profile for QIP, clinical audit, and patient-safety incident learning. Use local Trust, Board, ICB, or national specialty standards where they apply.
use_for: NHS clinical audit and QIP planning
UK source prompts for audit standards

sources.title: Best Practice in Clinical Audit
sources.use: clinical audit cycle, standards, measurement, and re-audit discipline

sources.title: A guide to quality improvement tools
sources.use: overview of QI tools including clinical audit, PDSA, Model for Improvement, Lean/Six Sigma, benchmarking, process mapping, SPC, and root-cause analysis

sources.title: Improvement resources
sources.use: NHS improvement resource directory including improvement guides, data resources, QI toolkits, huddle sheets, and training links

sources.title: Resources
sources.organisation: National Quality Improvement (incl. Clinical Audit) Network
sources.use: UK clinical audit and QI network resource directory linking HQIP, Making Data Count, Model Health System, Q, Health Foundation, CQC, GIRFT, and clinical audit support resources

sources.title: Patient Safety Incident Response Framework
sources.use: UK patient-safety incident response and learning framework

sources.title: PSIRF supporting guidance
sources.use: proportionate response, involvement, oversight, standards, policy, and plan templates

sources.title: Model for Improvement
sources.use: portable QI method used alongside NHS local governance

incident_learning.position: PSIRF belongs in a UK incident-learning module. It can generate improvement priorities and actions, but it should not be merged into the generic QIP core because it is jurisdiction-specific and governance-sensitive.
boundaries: do not process identifiable incident narratives in this kit
keep PSIRF narratives inside approved NHS systems
do not infer PSIRF response type from sparse chat notes
separate learning response, improvement action, and QIP measurement plan
